sources.title: Welteislehre - Wikipedia
Why Hitler and other Nazis thought the world was really made of ice - Big Think
Hanns Horbiger - RationalWiki

evidence_for.text: Nazi leadership including Hitler and Himmler embraced Welteislehre as official cosmological doctrine, demonstrating its cultural influence in the Third Reich.

evidence_against.text: The theory is universally rejected by modern astronomy and physics as pseudoscience with no observational support.

sources.title: Cosmic censorship hypothesis - Wikipedia
Mathematicians Disprove Conjecture Made to Save Black Holes - Quanta Magazine
The Strong Cosmic Censorship Conjecture - arXiv

evidence_for.text: Roger Penrose proposed in 1969 that singularities formed during gravitational collapse are always hidden behind event horizons; the conjecture remains one of the most important unsolved problems in general relativity.

evidence_against.text: Mathematicians have disproved specific versions of the conjecture under certain physical conditions, showing naked singularities can form in models of rotating charged black holes.

research_note: No external coverage found; speculative extension of the Cosmic Censorship Hypothesis concept — iceberg-chart lore only.

sources.title: Forest swastika - Wikipedia
Planted in 1933, this mysterious forest swastika remained unnoticed until 1992 - The Vintage News
Horticultural Hate: The Mystery of the Forest Swastikas - ABC News

evidence_for.text: The forest swastika is confirmed real: 100 larch trees planted circa 1938 near Zernikow, Brandenburg, Germany, visible from the air each autumn when their yellow-brown needles contrasted with surrounding pines; discovered in aerial photographs in 1992.

evidence_against.text: The location is Germany (Brandenburg), not Russia as sometimes misreported; authorities removed most trees in 1995 and 2000 to prevent it becoming a neo-Nazi pilgrimage site.

sources.title: What causes sinkholes? A Spiritual Perspective - Spiritual Science Research Foundation
Archon (Gnosticism) - Wikipedia
Archon - Britannica

evidence_for.text: Archons are well-documented theological entities in ancient Gnostic texts such as the Hypostasis of the Archons (Nag Hammadi, 2nd-3rd century CE), described as seven planetary rulers who obstruct human spiritual ascent.

sources.title: The Road to Roota - Federal Reserve Bank of Boston - Internet Archive
The Road to Roota Theory - roadtoroota.com
New York Fed Educational Comic Books

evidence_for.text: The Federal Reserve Bank of Boston published a children's comic ('Wishes and Rainbows', 1981) and an accompanying teacher's guide titled 'The Road to Roota'; this unusual publication is the genuine seed of the conspiracy theory.

evidence_against.text: The Federal Reserve openly acknowledges all its educational comic books; no evidence supports the interpretation that they encode a secret plan to return to a gold standard.

sources.title: The Ongoing Mystery of Arnold Schwarzenegger's Actual Height - Mental Floss
Arnold Height Truthers Think the Governator Is Telling a Very Tall Tale - MEL Magazine

evidence_for.text: Multiple journalists and measurements produced height estimates ranging from 5'6" to 6'3"; Men's Health concluded he was 5'10" rather than his claimed 6'2", and critic Roger Ebert reportedly disputed the official figure.

sources.title: Introspection Rundown - Wikipedia
Death of Lisa McPherson - Wikipedia
Scientology cult Introspection Rundown in the wrongful death of Lisa McPherson - WikiLeaks

evidence_for.text: Lisa McPherson died in 1995 after 17 days under the Introspection Rundown at Scientology's Flag Land Base; autopsy found severe dehydration and a pulmonary embolism consistent with prolonged enforced bed rest and isolation.

sources.title: Temporary Autonomous Zone - Wikipedia
T.A.Z.: The Temporary Autonomous Zone - The Anarchist Library

evidence_for.text: The TAZ concept has been practically implemented in autonomous zones, rave communities, and protest encampments worldwide since Hakim Bey's 1991 book; the notion of temporary spaces evading state control is documented across multiple political movements.

sources.title: Deep ecology - Wikipedia
Deep Ecology - Britannica
No, the NSA Did Not Invent Bitcoin - Bitcoin News
NSA Paper Debunked As Bitcoin Origin Theory - ainvest
Did the NSA Invent Bitcoin? A 1996 Research Paper Reignites the Debate - Bitcoin News

evidence_against.text: The 1996 NSA paper describes centralized e-cash schemes and does not propose Bitcoin's decentralized blockchain architecture; no credible link between the NSA and Satoshi Nakamoto's identity has ever been established.

research_note: No external coverage found; iceberg-chart lore only.

sources.title: Hermeneutics - Wikipedia
Hermeneutics (Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy)

evidence_for.text: Hermeneutics is a well-established academic discipline documented in the Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy, covering the methodology of interpretation in theology, law, and literary criticism.

sources.title: Negentropy - Wikipedia

evidence_against.text: Schrodinger's negentropy concept describes how living organisms maintain order by exporting entropy; it does not permit perpetual motion or infinite energy — the Second Law of Thermodynamics remains inviolable.

sources.title: Ro-langs - Wikipedia
Ro-Langs: The Risen Dead of the Himalayas - Historic Mysteries

evidence_for.text: Ro-langs (risen corpses) are genuine documented folklore entities in Tibetan, Bhutanese, and Sikkimese tradition, appearing in religious texts and historical accounts of dark sorcery.

sources.title: Abandoned Lifeboat on Bouvet Island: Mystery Solved! - Historic Mysteries
An abandoned lifeboat at world's end - Mike Dash History
Bouvet Island - Wikipedia

evidence_for.text: An abandoned, unmarked lifeboat was genuinely discovered on the remote Bouvet Island lagoon by HMS Protector in April 1964 with no trace of survivors; Bouvet is the world's most remote island.

evidence_against.text: The most credible explanation is that the lifeboat was abandoned by the Soviet whaling flotilla Slava during an emergency evacuation caused by an unexpected hurricane in November 1958.

sources.title: Does America Have a Secret Kangaroo Population? - Atlas Obscura
Cryptid Profile: Phantom Kangaroos - Pine Barrens Institute

evidence_against.text: Most documented phantom kangaroo cases are traceable to escaped exotic animals; some Midwestern sightings resulted in actual kangaroos being captured and sent to zoos, deflating the cryptid or conspiracy explanation.

sources.title: Patomskiy crater - Wikipedia
The Patomskiy Crater Mystery - Discovery UK

evidence_for.text: The Patomskiy crater is a real, scientifically-documented formation in Irkutsk, Siberia; dendrochronology confirmed it is approximately 300-350 years old and its cone-shaped limestone structure has no direct conventional geological analogue.

evidence_against.text: Three scientific expeditions concluded the most likely formation mechanism is underground methane or hydrogen gas emissions, not supernatural or extraterrestrial causes.

sources.title: Dighton Rock - Wikipedia
Who Made the Petroglyphs on the Mysterious Dighton Rock? - Ancient Origins

evidence_for.text: Dighton Rock is a confirmed historical artifact in Berkley, Massachusetts with documented petroglyphs first recorded in 1680; the intentional nature of the carvings is not disputed — only the authorship.

evidence_against.text: Mainstream archaeologists attribute the carvings to local Wampanoag Native Americans; similar indigenous petroglyphs are documented throughout the northeastern United States.

sources.title: Manichaeism - Wikipedia
Manichaeism - Britannica

evidence_for.text: Manichaeism was a historically significant world religion founded by the prophet Mani (~216-274 CE) that syncretized elements of Christianity, Zoroastrianism, and Buddhism; at its height it spread from North Africa to China.

sources.title: VALIS (novel) - Wikipedia

evidence_for.text: Philip K. Dick's 1981 novel VALIS draws directly from his documented '2-3-74' mystical experiences; Dick spent approximately 8,000 pages in his Exegesis analyzing whether these visions were divine, alien, or neurological in origin.

research_note: Broad theological concept with no distinct conspiracy-theory coverage; general Christian eschatology and free-will debate rather than a discrete iceberg entry.
No external coverage found; iceberg-chart lore only.

sources.title: Bigfoot and Portals: Unveiling the Interdimensional Theory - Connect Paranormal
Bigfoot and UFO Sightings Often Coincide - Gaia

evidence_against.text: No scientific evidence exists for interdimensional travel; mainstream cryptozoologists consider standard physical-animal hypotheses far more parsimonious than dimensional portal theories.

sources.title: Black helicopter - Wikipedia
Black helicopters - RationalWiki

evidence_against.text: Black unmarked helicopters used by US law enforcement and military agencies (CBP, DEA, FBI) are extensively documented; no evidence supports alien or biological-organism origin claims for these aircraft.

sources.title: Dominion theology - Wikipedia
Christian Dominionism and Pat Robertson's Shadow Government - Christian Democrats of America
Blood type personality theory - Wikipedia
ABO Blood Type and Personality Traits in Healthy Japanese Subjects - PMC

evidence_against.text: A 2015 PMC study of healthy Japanese subjects found no significant association between ABO blood type and personality traits, consistent with the scientific consensus that the theory lacks empirical support.

sources.title: The Controversial Dashka Stone: 120 Million-Year-Old Map? - Ancient Origins
Is The Dashka Stone A Hoax? - Weird History Facts

evidence_for.text: The Dashka Stone, discovered by Bashkir State University researchers in 1999, appears to show detailed topographic features of the Ural region including rivers and channels; its origins remain contested.

evidence_against.text: No reliable dating methodology has been published for the artifact; the 120-million-year age claim has no peer-reviewed support and many researchers consider it a hoax or misidentification of natural rock formations.

sources.title: Forbidden Archeology - Wikipedia
No, There Isn't a Conspiracy to Hide the Truth about Ancient History - Tales of Times Forgotten

evidence_against.text: Academic archaeologists actively benefit from discovering paradigm-changing evidence; institutional incentives run counter to systematic suppression, making a global cover-up of archaeological evidence implausible.

sources.title: China Is Hiding A Population Secret, Analyst Claims - Newsweek
How Reliable Are China's Statistics? - The Diplomat
China's population numbers are almost certainly inflated - South China Morning Post

evidence_for.text: Demographer Yi Fuxian (University of Wisconsin-Madison) documented systematic discrepancies in China's census data; tuberculosis vaccination records for newborns implied ~9.9 million births in 2018 vs. the official 15.23 million.

sources.title: Ghost Radio - Phoenix New Times
KCDX - Wikipedia

evidence_for.text: KCDX (103.1 FM) is a real FCC-licensed radio station broadcasting from Globe/Florence, Arizona, airing obscure album rock with no DJ, no commercials, and an owner (Ted Tucker) who deliberately maintained public mystery about his motivations.

evidence_against.text: KCDX is a legitimate FCC-licensed commercial station; its unusual format and secretive owner are documented but entirely legal and explicable as a personal broadcasting project.

sources.title: Satanic panic - Wikipedia
Blood libel - Wikipedia

evidence_against.text: The Satanic panic produced over 12,000 claims of ritual abuse in the US during the 1980s-90s; investigators found no material evidence of organized cult-based human sacrifice in any of the cases.

sources.title: Unethical human experimentation in the United States - Wikipedia
The U.S. Government's Secret Experimentation with Biological and Chemical Warfare - nospray.org

evidence_for.text: Between 1949 and 1974 the US government conducted hundreds of biological weapons tests on unsuspecting populations, including spraying bacterial tracers over cities and releasing agents in rural areas of Alaska.

evidence_against.text: While documented secret rural experiments did occur, there is no credible evidence they involved cryptid creation; the cryptid-correlation element of this theory is unsupported.

research_note: No external coverage found; iceberg-chart lore only.

sources.title: Transhumanism - Wikipedia
Super-intelligence and eternal life: transhumanism's faithful follow it blindly into a future for the elite - The Conversation
Brain in a vat - Wikipedia
Brain in a Vat Argument - Internet Encyclopedia of Philosophy

evidence_for.text: Hilary Putnam's 1981 formalization of the brain-in-a-vat scenario is a cornerstone of analytic epistemology; the scenario is logically unfalsifiable by design, which is why it continues to attract serious philosophical attention.

sources.title: Filter bubble - Wikipedia
Through the Newsfeed Glass: Rethinking Filter Bubbles and Echo Chambers - PMC

evidence_for.text: Filter bubble research by Eli Pariser (2011) and subsequent studies confirm algorithmic personalization produces demonstrably different content streams for different users, consistent with the 'segregated internet' observation.

evidence_against.text: Empirical studies show social media users generally encounter a more centrist, diverse content diet than offline audiences; the claim that segregation is intentionally engineered by elites to prevent unified opposition lacks direct evidence.

sources.title: Human cannibalism - Wikipedia
Why Is Cannibalism Taboo Across Almost Every Culture? - ScienceInsights

evidence_against.text: The cannibalism taboo is biologically adaptive: prion diseases like kuru are transmitted through consumption of human brain tissue, providing a clear evolutionary explanation for the taboo independent of any government conspiracy.

sources.title: The Cosmic Butterfly (Rosalind Ballingal) - Iceberg Database

research_note: Coverage limited to iceberg charting databases; no mainstream news or independent coverage found.

sources.title: Bogdanov affair - Wikipedia
Bogdanoff Twins - Know Your Meme

evidence_for.text: The Bogdanov twins (Igor and Grichka) were embroiled in a genuine academic scandal when their physics PhD theses were found to contain nonsensical but peer-review-passing content; a CNRS internal report concluded the theses had no scientific value.

evidence_against.text: The 'Bogpill' internet meme claiming the brothers control world finance and are archangels is a 4chan ironic conspiracy meme with no factual basis; the brothers were science TV hosts, not shadow rulers.

sources.title: The politics of the NPC meme - SAGE Journals
How The NPC Meme Tries To Dehumanize SJWs - Kotaku

evidence_against.text: Researchers identify the NPC meme as a dehumanization strategy from reactionary internet subcultures; the soulless-false-flag-agents extension has no evidentiary basis.

sources.title: United States Navy Marine Mammal Program - Wikipedia
The CIA's Most Highly-Trained Spies Weren't Even Human - Smithsonian Magazine
Military marine mammal - Wikipedia

evidence_for.text: The US Navy Marine Mammal Program (NMMP), begun in 1960, is a publicly acknowledged program using trained dolphins and sea lions for mine detection and harbor security, deployed in the Vietnam and Iraq wars.
The CIA's Cold War Project OXYGAS trained dolphins as ship-saboteurs; multiple declassified animal espionage programs are documented in Smithsonian research.

sources.title: Dark City (1998 film) - Wikipedia

evidence_against.text: Dark City is a documented 1998 science fiction film by director Alex Proyas; no credible evidence supports treating its alien memory-manipulation plot as literally non-fictional.

sources.title: Memetic warfare - Wikipedia
Information hazards - Memetics - Miraheze
Memetic Warfare: What's Real and How It Works - Fordham Political Review

evidence_for.text: Memetic warfare is a documented concept in psychological operations; a 2005 US Marine Corps dissertation explicitly discussed weaponizing memes to embed ideas into adversary populations.

evidence_against.text: The specific claim that hidden codes in images can trigger targeted emotional states as a 'virus' lacks neurological support; subliminal advertising studies consistently show minimal population-scale effects.
